sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_arrays (id INT, vec1 FLOAT[], vec2 FLOAT[] NOT NULL, vec3 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (1, array_value(1.0::FLOAT, 2.0::FLOAT, 3.0::FLOAT), array_value(2.0::FLOAT, 3.0::FLOAT, NULL));
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (2, array_value(4.0::FLOAT, 5.0::FLOAT, 6.0::FLOAT), array_value(NULL, 8.0::FLOAT, 9.0::FLOAT));
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (3, array_value(1.0::FLOAT, 1.0::FLOAT, 1.0::FLOAT), array_value(1.0::FLOAT, 1.0::FLOAT, 1.0::FLOAT));
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (4, array_value(0.0::FLOAT, 0.0::FLOAT, 0.0::FLOAT), array_value(5.0::FLOAT, 5.0::FLOAT, 5.0::FLOAT));
INSERT INTO test_arrays VALUES (5, array_value(-1.0::FLOAT, -2.0::FLOAT, -3.0::FLOAT), array_value(-2.0::FLOAT, -3.0::FLOAT, -4.0::FLOAT));
SELECT id, array_cosine_similarity(vec1, vec2) AS cosine_sim FROM test_arrays WHERE vec2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (array_cosine_similarity(vec1, vec2) <=> array_value(0.0::FLOAT, 0.0::FLOAT, 0.0::FLOAT)) AS distance FROM test_arrays HAVING distance IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, array_cosine_similarity(vec1, (SELECT vec2 FROM test_arrays WHERE id = 1)) AS sim_subquery FROM test_arrays;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS count_sim, ARRAY_AGG(array_cosine_similarity(vec1, vec2)) AS all_sims FROM test_arrays GROUP BY id HAVING count_sim > 1;
SELECT id, vec1, vec2, array_cosine_similarity(vec1, vec2) AS cosine_sim FROM test_arrays WHERE vec1[1] <= 0.0;
